name: Bubber Miley
description: American jazz trumpet and cornet player, composer (1903-1932)
abstract: James Wesley "Bubber" Miley (April 3, 1903 – May 20, 1932) was an American early jazz trumpet and cornet player, specializing in the use of the plunger mute.

about_quotes.speaker: Mary Lou Williams
about_quotes.evidence: I played in the band with Tricky Sam, Bubber Miley, and Sonny Greer

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: Bubber and Tricky would turn it around. We still played sweet music but we -- little by little it come to the growl and things, you know, because Tricky growled and Bubber growled

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: "Then I had old Bubber Miley on trumpet." and "I had my band together. Otto, Big Green, Bubber Miley and the whole band."

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: we hired Bubber Miley... Bubber Miley was the other -- when they put the second trumpet on they put Bubber Miley in there... Bubber was the main stay. He created the style for that band, he and Charlie Ervis.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Bubber Miley was one who created the utilization of the plunger... So that was one of the things that those of us who were around Ellington for a while, we learned a lot about that too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I know the Ellington band – Ellington used to keep people like Bubber Miley and other folks who were not readers – basically, ear players – in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
